events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a locates & marking compliance check at an industrial site. The key issue is that the critical label, torque sheet, or clearance dimension needed for the referenced requirement is outside the frame of the photograph. Only one angle is available, and the critical information needed to verify compliance is not visible.

Given that the critical information needed to make a determination is missing/outside the frame, the appropriate decision is "needs_more_info" rather than pass or fail. We cannot confirm compliance or non-compliance without seeing the critical label/dimension.

Let me construct the JSON response.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "needs_more_info",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.4,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Critical label, torque sheet, or clearance dimension referenced for compliance is outside the photograph frame and cannot be verified",
    "Only one camera angle provided — insufficient to confirm locate markings are complete, legible, and correctly offset per 811/CGA best practices",
    "Cannot confirm whether underground utility markings are present, color-coded correctly, or maintained within tolerance from the referenced work"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-photograph the area from additional angles capturing the referenced label, torque sheet, and clearance dimension",
    "Verify locate markings are on-site, legible, color-coded per APWA/811 standard, and within required tolerance of the referenced work",
    "Confirm locate ticket is current and not expired before any excavation or backfill continues"
  ],
  "rationale": "The work is at rough-complete stage, but the single photograph angle does not capture the critical label, torque sheet, or clearance dimension needed to verify compliance with 811/CGA best practices for locates and marking. Without this evidence, compliance cannot be confirmed or denied, so needs_more_info is the appropriate call.",
  "references": [
    "811/CGA Best Practices — Section on locating and marking",
    "APWA Uniform Color Code for underground utility lines"
  ]
}
